info.name: Telemax External API Tests
info.description:
Postman collection for Telemax API v1/v2 — 71 test cases across 8 modules (AUTH, DTC, WHK, VIN, POS, ALRT, ENG, GEN).

All TCs follow TESTCASE_RULES.md:
- TC ID: ModuleName-NNN
- Title: Verify [result] when [condition]
- Status default: Not Run
- Priority: High ≥30% per module

Base URL: https://api.telemax.com.au | Auth: Bearer token (~86399s lifetime)

IMPORTANT after import:
1. Fill in collection variable 'password'
2. Fill in deviceId, cleanDeviceId, movingDeviceId, otherCompanyDeviceId, testVin, testImei, alertId, webhookId, positionId
3. Run AUTH-001 first to refresh the token
4. The 'token' variable is pre-filled with the provided bearer token

exec: body: { mode: 'raw', raw: JSON.stringify({ email, password }) }
if (err) { console.error('[Auth] Refresh failed:', err); return; }

request.description:
TC ID: AUTH-001 | Title: Verify token is returned when valid credentials are submitted
Type: Functional | Priority: High | Status: Not Run | Environment: API — REST v1

TC ID: AUTH-002 | Title: Verify 401 is returned when an incorrect password is submitted
Type: Negative | Priority: High | Status: Not Run | Environment: API — REST v1

TC ID: AUTH-003 | Title: Verify 400 is returned when the email field is omitted from the token request
Type: Negative | Priority: Medium | Status: Not Run | Environment: API — REST v1

item.name: AUTH-004 – GET protected endpoint (no Authorization header)

request.description:
TC ID: AUTH-004 | Title: Verify 401 is returned when a protected endpoint is accessed without an Authorization header
Type: Security | Priority: High | Status: Not Run | Environment: API — REST v1

TC ID: AUTH-005 | Title: Verify 401 is returned when a malformed Bearer token is supplied
Type: Security | Priority: High | Status: Not Run | Environment: API — REST v1

TC ID: AUTH-006 | Title: Verify 401 is returned when an expired token is used on a protected endpoint
Type: Security | Priority: High | Status: Not Run | Environment: API — REST v1

item.name: AUTH-007 – GET internal rebuild cache endpoint (must not be publicly accessible)

request.description:
TC ID: AUTH-007 | Title: Verify the internal rebuild vehicle movement cache endpoint is not publicly accessible
Type: Security | Priority: High | Status: Not Run | Environment: API — REST v1
Notes: 200 response = security vulnerability.

exec: pm.test('DTC-001: description populated (regression)', () => { const a=pm.response.json(); if(a.length>0) pm.expect(a[0].description).to.not.be.empty; });
pm.test('DTC-001: severity populated (regression)', () => { const a=pm.response.json(); if(a.length>0) pm.expect(a[0].severity).to.not.be.undefined; });

request.description:
TC ID: DTC-001 | Title: Verify DTC list is returned when a valid device identifier is supplied
Type: Functional | Priority: High | Status: Not Run | Environment: API — REST v1
Notes: description and severity were previously unpopulated — regression guard.

TC ID: DTC-002 | Title: Verify 404 is returned when requesting DTCs for a device belonging to another company
Type: Security | Priority: High | Status: Not Run | Environment: API — REST v1
Notes: Must be 404 NOT 403 — prevents company enumeration.

TC ID: DTC-003 | Title: Verify 400 is returned when the deviceId query parameter is omitted
Type: Negative | Priority: Medium | Status: Not Run | Environment: API — REST v1

TC ID: DTC-004 | Title: Verify 404 is returned when the supplied device identifier does not exist
Type: Negative | Priority: Medium | Status: Not Run | Environment: API — REST v1

item.name: DTC-005 – GET /api/v1/dtc (device with no DTCs returns empty array)

request.description:
TC ID: DTC-005 | Title: Verify empty array is returned when device has no recorded diagnostic trouble codes
Type: Edge Case | Priority: Medium | Status: Not Run | Environment: API — REST v1

exec: pm.test('DTC-006: description populated (regression)', () => { const a=pm.response.json(); if(a.length>0) pm.expect(a[0].description).to.not.be.empty; });

request.description:
TC ID: DTC-006 | Title: Verify DTC list is returned from the version 2 endpoint when a valid device identifier is supplied

TC ID: DTC-007 | Title: Verify 401 is returned when requesting diagnostic trouble codes without an Authorization header
Type: Security | Priority: High | Status: Not Run | Environment: API — REST v1

TC ID: DTC-008 | Title: Verify the DTC endpoint responds within 2000 milliseconds for a standard device request

TC ID: DTC-009 | Title: Verify 400 is returned when the deviceId parameter is present but empty

request.description:

exec: pm.test('WHK-001: Each item has id and url', () => { const a=pm.response.json(); if(a.length>0){ pm.expect(a[0]).to.have.property('id'); pm.expect(a[0]).to.have.property('url'); } });

exec: pm.test('WHK-002: Response has id', () => { const j=pm.response.json(); pm.expect(j).to.have.property('id'); pm.collectionVariables.set('webhookId', j.id); });

request.description:

Type: Functional | Priority: High | Status: Not Run | Environment: API — REST v1

item.name: WHK-006 – POST /api/v1/webhooks (HMAC secret stored and signed delivery)

exec: pm.test('WHK-006: Response does not expose raw secret', () => { const j=pm.response.json(); pm.expect(j.secret||'').to.not.equal('supersecret_hmac_key'); });

request.description:
TC ID: WHK-006 | Title: Verify webhook is created with HMAC secret and signature header is expected on delivery

exec: pm.test('WHK-008: Response is empty or confirms deletion', () => { const t=pm.response.text(); pm.expect(t.length).to.be.below(500); });

request.description:

exec: pm.test('WHK-013: Delivery acknowledged', () => { const j=pm.response.json(); pm.expect(j).to.exist; });

request.description:
TC ID: WHK-013 | Title: Verify a test delivery is dispatched when the webhook test endpoint is triggered
Type: Functional | Priority: Medium | Status: Not Run | Environment: API — REST v1

exec: pm.test('WHK-014: All items belong to own company', () => { const a=pm.response.json(); a.forEach(w => pm.expect(String(w.companyId||pm.collectionVariables.get('companyId'))).to.equal(pm.collectionVariables.get('companyId'))); });

TC ID: VIN-001 | Title: Verify vehicle details are returned when a valid VIN is supplied

item.name: VIN-002 – GET /api/v1/vehicles/vin/{vin} (VIN from another company returns 404)

request.description:
TC ID: VIN-002 | Title: Verify 404 is returned when looking up a VIN belonging to another company
Type: Security | Priority: High | Status: Not Run | Environment: API — REST v1
Notes: Must be 404 not 403 — prevents VIN enumeration across companies.

TC ID: VIN-003 | Title: Verify 404 is returned when the requested VIN does not exist in the system
Type: Negative | Priority: Medium | Status: Not Run | Environment: API — REST v1

exec: pm.test('VIN-004: Response has deviceId or imei', () => { const j=pm.response.json(); pm.expect(j.imei||j.deviceId).to.exist; });

request.description:

request.description:

Type: Negative | Priority: Medium | Status: Not Run | Environment: API — REST v1

item.name: VIN-008 – GET /api/v1/vehicles/vin/{vin} (response includes ConnectionStrengh field with original spelling)

exec: pm.test('VIN-008: ConnectionStrengh field present (intentional misspelling)', () => { const j=pm.response.json(); pm.expect(j).to.have.property('ConnectionStrengh'); });
pm.test('VIN-008: ConnectionStrength (corrected) must NOT exist', () => { const j=pm.response.json(); pm.expect(j).to.not.have.property('ConnectionStrength'); });

request.description:
TC ID: VIN-008 | Title: Verify response includes the ConnectionStrengh field using the intentional original spelling
Type: Regression | Priority: High | Status: Not Run | Environment: API — REST v1
Notes: ConnectionStrengh (missing 't') is intentional — must NOT be renamed to ConnectionStrength.

item.name: VIN-009 – GET /api/v1/vehicles/vin/{vin} (response includes UserTimeFormated field with original spelling)

request.description:
TC ID: VIN-009 | Title: Verify response includes the UserTimeFormated field using the intentional original spelling
Type: Regression | Priority: High | Status: Not Run | Environment: API — REST v1
Notes: UserTimeFormated (single 't') is intentional — must NOT be renamed.

item.name: VIN-010 – GET /api/v1/vehicles (list all vehicles for company)

request.description:
TC ID: VIN-010 | Title: Verify all vehicles belonging to the authenticated company are returned
Type: Functional | Priority: High | Status: Not Run | Environment: API — REST v1

exec: pm.test('POS-001: Each position has lat and lng', () => { const a=pm.response.json(); if(a.length>0){ pm.expect(a[0]).to.have.property('lat'); pm.expect(a[0]).to.have.property('lng'); } });
pm.test('POS-001: Result count does not exceed limit', () => pm.expect(pm.response.json().length).to.be.at.most(10));

request.description:
TC ID: POS-001 | Title: Verify position history is returned when a valid device identifier and limit are supplied
Type: Functional | Priority: High | Status: Not Run | Environment: API — REST v1

item.name: POS-002 – GET /api/v1/positions (device from another company returns 404)

request.description:
TC ID: POS-002 | Title: Verify 404 is returned when requesting positions for a device belonging to another company
Type: Security | Priority: High | Status: Not Run | Environment: API — REST v1
Notes: Must be 404 not 403.

TC ID: POS-003 | Title: Verify 400 is returned when the deviceId query parameter is omitted from a position request
Type: Negative | Priority: Medium | Status: Not Run | Environment: API — REST v1

exec: const a=pm.response.json(); const from=new Date('2024-01-01T00:00:00Z'); const to=new Date('2024-01-02T00:00:00Z');
a.forEach(p => { if(p.timestamp){ const t=new Date(p.timestamp); pm.expect(t.getTime()).to.be.within(from.getTime(), to.getTime()); } });

request.description:
TC ID: POS-004 | Title: Verify only positions within the specified date range are returned
Type: Functional | Priority: Medium | Status: Not Run | Environment: API — REST v1

TC ID: POS-005 | Title: Verify appropriate response is returned when the limit parameter is set to zero
Type: Edge Case | Priority: Low | Status: Not Run | Environment: API — REST v1

TC ID: POS-006 | Title: Verify only the most recent position is returned when limit is set to one
Type: Functional | Priority: Medium | Status: Not Run | Environment: API — REST v1

TC ID: POS-007 | Title: Verify 401 is returned when requesting positions without an Authorization header
Type: Security | Priority: High | Status: Not Run | Environment: API — REST v1

TC ID: POS-008 | Title: Verify the positions endpoint responds within 3000 milliseconds for a 100-record request
Type: Performance | Priority: Low | Status: Not Run | Environment: API — REST v1

exec: pm.test('ALRT-001: Each alert has id and type', () => { const a=pm.response.json(); if(a.length>0){ pm.expect(a[0]).to.have.property('id'); pm.expect(a[0]).to.have.property('type'); } });

request.description:
TC ID: ALRT-001 | Title: Verify all alerts are returned when listing alerts for the authenticated company
Type: Functional | Priority: High | Status: Not Run | Environment: API — REST v1

exec: pm.test('ALRT-002: isRead is true in response', () => { const j=pm.response.json(); if(j.isRead!==undefined) pm.expect(j.isRead).to.equal(true); });

request.description:
TC ID: ALRT-002 | Title: Verify alert is marked as read when the read endpoint is called with a valid alert identifier
Type: Functional | Priority: High | Status: Not Run | Environment: API — REST v1

item.name: ALRT-003 – POST /api/v1/alerts/{id}/read with isRead=false (known bug: always marks as read)

exec: pm.test('ALRT-003: Alert is still read (isRead=false is no-op regression)', () => {

request.description:
TC ID: ALRT-003 | Title: Verify SetAlertAsRead always marks the alert as read regardless of the isRead parameter value
Type: Regression | Priority: High | Status: Not Run | Environment: API — REST v1
Notes: Known bug — isRead=false is a no-op. Alert is always marked as read. This test guards against accidental fix changing the contract.

TC ID: ALRT-004 | Title: Verify 404 is returned when marking as read an alert identifier that does not exist
Type: Negative | Priority: Medium | Status: Not Run | Environment: API — REST v1

exec: pm.test('ALRT-005: All alerts belong to own company', () => { const a=pm.response.json(); a.forEach(al => { pm.expect(String(al.companyId||pm.collectionVariables.get('companyId'))).to.equal(pm.collectionVariables.get('companyId')); }); });

request.description:
TC ID: ALRT-005 | Title: Verify alerts belonging to another company are not included in the authenticated company alert listing
Type: Security | Priority: High | Status: Not Run | Environment: API — REST v1

TC ID: ALRT-006 | Title: Verify 401 is returned when listing alerts without an Authorization header
Type: Security | Priority: High | Status: Not Run | Environment: API — REST v1

exec: pm.test('ALRT-007: All returned alerts are unread', () => { const a=pm.response.json(); a.forEach(al => { if(al.isRead!==undefined) pm.expect(al.isRead).to.equal(false); }); });

request.description:
TC ID: ALRT-007 | Title: Verify only unread alerts are returned when the isRead filter is set to false
Type: Functional | Priority: Medium | Status: Not Run | Environment: API — REST v1

TC ID: ENG-001 | Title: Verify engine disable command is accepted when the vehicle is not currently moving
Type: Functional | Priority: High | Status: Not Run | Environment: API — REST v1

exec: pm.test('ENG-002: SAFETY — Status 422 (cannot disable moving vehicle)', () => pm.response.to.have.status(422));

pm.test('ENG-002: Error message references vehicle movement', () => { const j=pm.response.json(); pm.expect(JSON.stringify(j).toLowerCase()).to.satisfy(s => s.includes('moving')||s.includes('speed')||s.includes('motion')); });

request.description:
TC ID: ENG-002 | Title: Verify 422 is returned when an engine disable command is issued while the vehicle is moving
Type: Security | Priority: High | Status: Not Run | Environment: API — REST v1
Notes: SAFETY-CRITICAL — engine disable while moving must be rejected with 422. This must NEVER return 200.

TC ID: ENG-003 | Title: Verify engine enable command is accepted when a valid device identifier is supplied
Type: Functional | Priority: High | Status: Not Run | Environment: API — REST v1

item.name: ENG-004 – POST /api/v1/devices/{id}/engine/disable (device from another company returns 404)

request.description:
TC ID: ENG-004 | Title: Verify 404 is returned when attempting to disable the engine of a device belonging to another company
Type: Security | Priority: High | Status: Not Run | Environment: API — REST v1
Notes: Must be 404 not 403 — prevents company enumeration.

TC ID: ENG-005 | Title: Verify 401 is returned when issuing an engine disable command without an Authorization header

TC ID: ENG-006 | Title: Verify 404 is returned when issuing an engine disable command for a device identifier that does not exist
Type: Negative | Priority: Medium | Status: Not Run | Environment: API — REST v1

Type: Functional | Priority: High | Status: Not Run | Environment: API — REST v1

item.name: GEN-003 – GET /api/v1/devices/{id} (device from another company returns 404)

request.description:
TC ID: GEN-003 | Title: Verify 404 is returned when requesting details for a device belonging to another company
Type: Security | Priority: High | Status: Not Run | Environment: API — REST v1
Notes: Must be 404 not 403.

TC ID: GEN-004 | Title: Verify 404 is returned when requesting details for a device identifier that does not exist
Type: Negative | Priority: Medium | Status: Not Run | Environment: API — REST v1

TC ID: GEN-005 | Title: Verify 401 is returned when listing devices without an Authorization header
Type: Security | Priority: High | Status: Not Run | Environment: API — REST v1

TC ID: GEN-006 | Title: Verify the device listing endpoint responds within 2000 milliseconds
Type: Performance | Priority: Low | Status: Not Run | Environment: API — REST v1

exec: pm.test('GEN-007: All devices scoped to own company', () => { const a=pm.response.json(); a.forEach(d => { pm.expect(String(d.companyId||pm.collectionVariables.get('companyId'))).to.equal(pm.collectionVariables.get('companyId')); }); });

request.description:
TC ID: GEN-007 | Title: Verify only devices belonging to the authenticated company are included in the device listing
Type: Security | Priority: High | Status: Not Run | Environment: API — REST v1

exec: pm.test('GEN-008: Response has company id', () => { const j=pm.response.json(); pm.expect(j.id||j.companyId).to.exist; });
pm.test('GEN-008: Company id matches expected', () => { const j=pm.response.json(); pm.expect(String(j.id||j.companyId)).to.equal(pm.collectionVariables.get('companyId')); });

request.description:
TC ID: GEN-008 | Title: Verify company profile details are returned when requesting the authenticated company profile
Type: Functional | Priority: Medium | Status: Not Run | Environment: API — REST v1
